summary: Matched and integrated a large slice of Yoshi fighter code around ftyoshi4 into C-side ftYoshi sources, deleting 812 lines from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftyoshi4.s and adding most of the implementation/prototypes to src/melee/ft/chara/ftYoshi/ftyoshi2.c/.h. The PR also fixed ftYoshi attribute field types from s32 to f32 for several offsets, added missing fighter/animation helper declarations, and moved a previously disabled func_8012C850 implementation from ftyoshi1.c into active ftyoshi2.c. Evidence shows a hybrid approach: several routines were written in C, while still-difficult routines were kept as MWERKS_GEKKO inline asm inside the C translation unit. The PR notes it depended on #713 and resolved #600, with co-authorship from Johnny Urosevic.

key_files.role: Deleted the standalone assembly body for this Yoshi slice, removing 812 lines.
Primary implementation target; gained C translations, helper extraction, and MWERKS_GEKKO inline asm for remaining hard-to-match routines.
Added prototypes for the newly integrated ftyoshi2 functions and included Vec3 type support via dolphin/mtx/types.h.
Removed a disabled #if false draft of func_8012C850 after moving/activating the implementation elsewhere.
Corrected ftYoshiAttributes field types and added cross-file prototypes for newly used ftyoshi routines.
Declared additional common fighter helper functions used by the matched Yoshi code.
Added declaration for func_8006FA58 used by the new ftyoshi implementation.

what_changed: Removed the large standalone assembly block from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftyoshi4.s.

Added implementations/prototypes for Yoshi functions and callbacks including func_8012C1D4, lbl_8012C2F4, lbl_8012C3EC, lbl_8012C45C, lbl_8012C47C, func_8012C49C, lbl_8012C54C, lbl_8012C59C, lbl_8012C5C0, lbl_8012C5E0, func_8012C600, lbl_8012C7A4, lbl_8012C80C, lbl_8012C810, lbl_8012C830, func_8012C850, lbl_8012CACC, and several later helpers through func_8012CD00.

Extracted repeated effect-spawn setup into a static inline helper named spawnEffect, covering the sequence around func_80074B0C, func_8007B0C0, func_8007500C, fighter bone lookup, and efAsync_Spawn with effect id 0x4CF.
Activated func_8012C850 in ftyoshi2.c and removed its disabled draft from ftyoshi1.c.
Changed ftYoshiAttributes fields x14, x1C, x20, and x24 from s32 to f32, aligning them with float use in functions such as func_8012CC94, func_8012CCE0, func_8012CCF0, and func_8012CD00.
Added declarations for common fighter helpers such as func_80092BE8, func_80093240, func_800932DC, func_8009370C, and func_80093BC0.
Added the func_8006FA58 prototype to ftanim.h.

Kept several routines as inline asm under MWERKS_GEKKO pragmas rather than leaving them in external asm, reducing separate asm file debt while preserving exact code generation where C was not yet matched.
smart_moves: Factored a duplicated Yoshi effect-spawn sequence into static inline spawnEffect, then reused it in both lbl_8012C030 and lbl_8012C2F4, reducing repeated C while preserving the call shape needed for matching.

Used a staged migration strategy: delete the external assembly file but keep stubborn routines as MWERKS_GEKKO inline asm in ftyoshi2.c, allowing nearby matched C, headers, and prototypes to progress without needing every function fully decompiled at once.
Corrected special-attribute field types to f32 where the code reads them as vectors/scalars, which makes later decompilation more semantically accurate and avoids repeated casts or mismatches.
Preserved compiler-sensitive details such as explicit unused locals, address-named extern constants like lbl_804D9A28/lbl_804D9A2C/lbl_804D9A30, and exact callback/function-pointer assignments.
Moved a previously disabled implementation of func_8012C850 into the active source file, removing stale #if false code from ftyoshi1.c instead of leaving duplicate draft logic.
decomp_lessons: For fighter action-state routines, matching often depends on exact call ordering around Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC, animation/physics helpers, state variables, and effect spawning.
Do not assume attribute fields are integers just because they are unnamed; usage in vector output or direct floating-point math is strong evidence for f32 field types.
Repeated assembly idioms around fighter bone lookup plus efAsync_Spawn can be extracted into a static inline helper when the compiler still emits compatible code.
Inline asm inside the C file is a useful intermediate state when removing a standalone asm file but not all routines are ready for high-level C.
Header cleanup is part of matching work: adding accurate prototypes for helper functions prevents local extern clutter and documents cross-module dependencies for future files.
Small empty callbacks such as lbl_8012C80C and lbl_8012CACC should still be represented explicitly when they are part of action-state callback tables or function-pointer flows.
naming_conventions: Kept unresolved functions and callbacks under address-based names such as func_8012C49C and lbl_8012C2F4.
Used HSD_GObj* fighter_gobj as the common parameter name for fighter object callbacks.
Used fp for Fighter* locals and ftYoshiAttributes* attr for special attributes.
Retained existing state-var style field names such as x234C_stateVar4_s32, x2354_stateVar6_f32, and x2358_stateVar7.
Used a local helper name spawnEffect rather than address-style naming because it is a new static inline abstraction, not an original symbol.
Preserved address-named constants such as lbl_804D9A28, lbl_804D9A2C, lbl_804D9A30, and lbl_804D9A38 where semantic names were not yet known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted the external ftyoshi4.s body and colocated remaining asm routines in ftyoshi2.c under #ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO, #pragma push/pop, and asm void wrappers.
Used explicit unused stack locals/arrays in C functions to influence stack layout and match register/stack behavior.
Kept exact float constants and extern labels rather than substituting literal 0.0f/1.0f everywhere, which helps preserve small-data references and instruction selection.
Used f64 lbl_804D9A30 in inline asm around integer-to-float conversion for func_8012C600, reflecting the original xoris/lfd conversion pattern.
Matched small wrapper callbacks as one-call C functions where possible, e.g. wrappers around func_80092B70, func_80092BAC, func_80092E10, and func_80092E30.
For routines that check ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining, preserved branch semantics using explicit zero comparisons or negation patterns as seen in the matched C.

review_feedback.note: No textual review comments were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace the MWERKS_GEKKO inline asm routines in ftyoshi2.c with full C.
Search for subsequent renames of func_8012C1D4, func_8012C49C, func_8012C600, and func_8012C850 to semantic Yoshi special-move names.
Check whether ftYoshiAttributes fields x14, x1C, x20, and x24 receive semantic names in later struct cleanup PRs.
Look for reuse of the Yoshi effect-spawn pattern around efAsync_Spawn effect id 0x4CF in other fighter decompilation work.
